name: Get-AutopilotDiagnosticsCommunity
description: A PowerShell diagnostic script for analyzing Windows Autopilot deployments. Provides comprehensive details about Autopilot profile settings, policies, apps, and certificate profiles tracked via Enrollment Status Page, with support for local PC analysis and captured log files.
keywords: why is autopilot taking so long
author: Andrew Taylor

securityCheck.aiSummary: Overall assessment: The codebase primarily implements backup/restore tooling with OneDrive integration, which can be legitimate in Intune/Autopilot contexts. However, there are notable security concerns: (1) broad data exfiltration to OneDrive/OneDriveCommercial without explicit consent or encryption controls; (2) hidden/invisible execution patterns via VBScript, which could enable unsupervised tasks; (3) brittle/erroneous variable usage in NEWrestore.bat that could lead to failed or misrouted restores; (4) registry data being logged/exported to external storage; and (5) no credential harvesting or hardcoded secrets detected. Recommend tightening governance: document data handling policy, obtain consent, implement signed/auditable scheduled tasks, ensure encryption, validate and fix faulty variable usage in restore scripts, and review registry-logging practices for privacy impact.

noRemoteExecution.reason: IEX with WebClient download in Powershell Scripts/AVD/avd-box-config-generic.ps1 ([lines: 224](https://github.com/andrew-s-taylor/public/blob/main/Powershell Scripts/AVD/avd-box-config-generic.ps1#L224))
